description: Synthesus Knowledge Cloud — Shared World Knowledge Repository

entries.description: Dragons are ancient, fearsome reptilian creatures of immense power. Legends say they once ruled the skies over the Northern Mountains before retreating into deep caverns beneath the Frostpeak range. [entity] is a [topic] creature of great [emotion] danger.
facts: Breathes fire hot enough to melt steel
Guards treasure hoards obsessively
Hates thieves above all other intruders
Can sense gold from miles away
Ancient dragons can speak in riddles

emotion_variants.afraid: Dragons... *shudders* Don't speak of them too loudly. The old tales say they can hear their name on the wind.
emotion_variants.friendly: Ah, dragons! Magnificent creatures in the old stories. Terrible, yes, but magnificent. My grandfather claimed he saw one flying over the Frostpeak range sixty years ago.

entries.description: Trolls are hulking, dim-witted creatures that dwell under bridges and in dark caves along trade routes. They're not especially dangerous alone, but a pack of them can overwhelm even experienced fighters. [entity] inspires [emotion] annoyance in [topic] encounters.
facts: Stone-hard skin that regenerates minor wounds
Turns to stone in direct sunlight
Attracted to the smell of cooked meat
Territorial but can be bribed with food

entries.description: Shadow Wraiths are spectral entities that manifest in places of great suffering or dark magic. They drain the warmth from the living and leave a cold that seeps into the bones. Some say the missing caravans in Blackhollow were taken by wraiths. [entity] fills victims with [emotion] terror in the [time] hours.
facts: Invisible in complete darkness
Can pass through solid walls
Leaves a trail of frost where it moves
Drawn to fear and despair

emotion_variants.afraid: *lowers voice* The wraiths... they say you don't see them, you feel them. A cold that creeps into your chest. By the time you notice, it's too late.

entries.description: Dire wolves are massive predators twice the size of their common cousins. They hunt in packs across the Northern Road and the forests beyond. Experienced hunters know to travel in groups when dire wolves are about. [entity] commands [emotion] respect in [topic] hunts.
facts: Hunt in coordinated packs with military precision
Alpha pairs mate for life
Can track prey across three days of rainfall
Their pelts are worth a small fortune

entries.description: Ironhaven is a bustling trade city built where the Northern Road meets the Silverflow River. Home to the Merchant's Alliance and governed by Duke Aldric, it serves as the commercial heart of the region with the busiest market this side of the mountains. [entity] thrives with [topic] activity in the [time] hours.
facts: The market square hosts traders from six surrounding regions
The Merchant's Alliance controls all commercial tariffs
Duke Aldric's castle overlooks the city from the northern hill
Famous for its ironwork and merchant guilds

entries.description: Silvermoor is an elegant city of white stone walls five days' ride south along the trade road. Famous throughout the realm for silk, enchanted fabrics, and exquisite dyes produced by the Silvermoor Weavers' Guild.
facts: The Weavers' Guild is the finest textile guild in the realm
White stone walls glow faintly at sunset
Home to the largest dye markets in three kingdoms
Enchanted fabrics from Silvermoor can resist fire and blade

entries.description: Blackhollow is a dark, dense stretch of forest on the Northern Road about two days' ride from Ironhaven. The trees grow thick and the road narrows dangerously. Three caravans have gone missing there in recent months, and merchants speak of shadows in the treeline. [entity] fills travelers with [emotion] dread in the [time] hours.
facts: No sunlight reaches the forest floor at midday
Local wildlife has fled the area in recent months
Ancient ruins are rumored to lie at its heart
Temperature drops noticeably upon entering

emotion_variants.afraid: Blackhollow... nobody goes there willingly anymore. Something dwells in those trees. Something that took three caravans without a trace.

entries.description: Frostpeak Pass is the treacherous mountain crossing north of the trade routes. Brutal in winter and barely passable even in summer, it connects the lowland kingdoms to the northern territories. You can see three kingdoms from its summit on a clear day.
facts: Avalanches claim lives every winter
You can see three kingdoms from the summit
Ancient waymarkers guide travelers through the fog
The pass has been used for trade for over 500 years

entries.description: The Northern Road is the primary trade artery running from Ironhaven through Blackhollow and up to the mountain passes. It's been the lifeline of commerce for the Merchant's Alliance for centuries, though recent caravan disappearances have made it increasingly dangerous. [entity] carries [topic] caravans through all [time] hours.
facts: Half the merchants in the Alliance depend on this road
Guard patrols have been doubled after the disappearances
Waystations every day's ride offer shelter and supplies

entries.description: Redstone is a rough mining town east of Ironhaven, built around rich deposits of iron, copper, and gemstones. The people are hard, the taverns are rowdy, and they pay premium prices for supplies from the lowlands.
facts: The mines run deep beneath the red clay hills
Gemstones from Redstone are prized across three kingdoms
Bar fights are considered a local sport

entries.description: The Merchant's Alliance is the powerful trade guild that controls commerce across the region. They negotiate tariffs with the duke, organize caravan escorts, settle trade disputes, and maintain the trade roads. Without them, the regional economy would collapse. [entity] manages [topic] operations around the clock in [time] hours.
facts: Controls all commercial tariffs in the region
Maintains a private guard force for caravan escorts
Settles trade disputes through binding arbitration
The Guild Master is elected by senior merchants

entries.description: Duke Aldric's personal military force responsible for maintaining order in Ironhaven and the surrounding territory. Well-equipped but stretched thin, they patrol the city walls and occasionally escort high-value caravans.
facts: Wear the duke's sigil — a silver tower on blue
Have jurisdiction over criminal matters
Recently increased patrols due to caravan disappearances
Underfunded due to declining tax revenue

entries.description: The finest textile guild in the realm, based in Silvermoor. Their enchanted fabrics and exquisite dyes are sought after by nobility across multiple kingdoms. Membership is earned through a seven-year apprenticeship.
facts: Their enchanted silk can resist fire and blade
Only accept 12 new apprentices per year
Master Weavers can imbue fabric with minor magical properties
Their trademark blue dye is a closely guarded secret

entries.description: Starfire Essence is a rare alchemical compound that glows with an ethereal inner light. Used in high-end enchantments and the creation of magical artifacts, it's worth more than its weight in gold. [entity] inspires [emotion] awe among [topic] practitioners.
facts: Glows with a warm, pulsing light even in complete darkness
Only found in deposits beneath ancient magical sites
Must be stored in lead-lined containers or it evaporates within days
A single vial can power an enchantment lasting decades

entries.description: Silvermoor Silk is the finest textile in the realm — lightweight, durable, and available in colors that never fade. The enchanted varieties woven by the Weavers' Guild can resist fire, repel water, and even mend small tears on their own. [entity] brings [emotion] delight to [topic] enthusiasts.
facts: Colors never fade even after years of wear
Enchanted silk can resist fire and repel water
Takes 6 months to weave a single enchanted bolt
Duke Aldric's court robes are made exclusively from it

entries.description: Healing potions are alchemical remedies that accelerate the body's natural recovery. Quality varies wildly — a good one from a trained herbalist can mend a broken bone in hours, while a cheap market potion might just cure a headache. [entity] provides [topic] relief when needed most in [time] hours.
facts: Made from moonpetal flowers and silverroot
Must be consumed within a month of brewing
Higher quality potions have a faintly golden color
Overconsumption causes temporary blindness

entries.description: Enchanting reagents are the raw materials required for imbuing objects with magical properties. They include crystallized mana, powdered moonstone, and various rare botanical extracts. Without them, even the most skilled enchanter is powerless.
facts: Crystallized mana loses potency after 90 days
Powdered moonstone must be harvested under a full moon
The best reagents come from deep underground deposits
Impure reagents can cause enchantments to backfire violently

entries.description: Three merchant caravans have vanished on the Northern Road in the past two months. Two were found empty — drivers reported being robbed by something fast and invisible. The third hasn't been found at all. The Merchant's Alliance is in crisis. The [entity] mystery creates [emotion] tension in [topic] circles.
facts: Drivers reported shadows moving impossibly fast
All disappearances occurred near Blackhollow
The Alliance is hiring sellswords for protection
Duke's Guard investigation has produced no results

entries.description: A devastating plague that swept through the region five years ago during an unusually harsh winter. It claimed hundreds of lives including many prominent citizens. The herbalists eventually developed a cure from frostbloom flowers. The [entity] created [emotion] despair in [topic] communities [time] ago.
facts: Struck hardest in overcrowded market districts
No noble family was spared
The cure was discovered by the herbalist Thessaly
Survivors gained natural immunity

entries.description: The network of roads and paths connecting the major cities and towns of the region. The Northern Road is the primary artery, but secondary routes connect Redstone, the coastal towns, and the mountain settlements. Trade routes are the lifeblood of the economy. [entity] facilitate [topic] movement in all [time] hours.
facts: Waystations every day's ride offer safe rest
Route guards are hired and paid by the Alliance
Winter closures can cause months of supply shortages
Bandits target the less-patrolled secondary routes

entries.description: The art of negotiation over prices — an essential skill in any marketplace. Experienced merchants know that the posted price is merely a starting point. Relationship, timing, and charm all factor into the final deal. [entity] is crucial in [topic] dealings, especially at [time] close.
facts: Most merchants expect customers to negotiate
Insulting lowball offers can permanently damage a relationship
Buying in bulk typically warrants a 10-15% discount
The best time to haggle is at market close when merchants want to sell remaining stock

entries.description: The magical art of imbuing objects with supernatural properties. Practiced by trained enchanters using specialized reagents, it ranges from simple glow-charms to complex battle enchantments that can turn the tide of war. [entity] inspires [emotion] wonder among [topic] students.
facts: Requires specific reagents matched to the desired effect
Poorly made enchantments can backfire catastrophically
The Silvermoor Weavers are the foremost textile enchanters
Military-grade enchantments are restricted by ducal law

entries.description: The body of rules and regulations governing commerce, guild membership, and trade disputes in the region. Enforced by the Merchant's Alliance with the backing of ducal authority, it covers everything from tariffs to quality standards. [entity] governs [topic] practices year-round in [time] hours.
facts: All merchants must pay annual guild dues
Price fixing is punishable by expulsion
Trade disputes are settled by guild arbitration
Non-guild merchants pay double tariffs at gates

entries.description: The Synthesus Engine is a dual-hemisphere cognitive architecture for NPC intelligence. The Left Hemisphere handles fast pattern matching while the Right Hemisphere manages emotion, memory, relationships, and world awareness. It replaces traditional LLM-based dialogue with a system running 90% on pattern matching.
facts: Runs 90% on pattern matching and 10% on specialized ML micro-models
Total ML footprint is approximately 458 KB
Can support 1000+ concurrent NPCs on a single server
Zero GPU required — runs entirely on CPU

entries.description: AIVM LLC is the company behind the Synthesus Engine — a startup focused on building intelligent NPC systems that don't require cloud-connected LLMs or GPU hardware. Their philosophy: games should run on the hardware players already own.
facts: Founded to solve the cost problem of LLM-based NPCs
Build systems that run entirely on consumer hardware
Developed the ML Swarm architecture of 12 specialized micro-models
Goal: make every NPC feel alive without a $60/month API bill

entries.description: The ML Swarm is a collection of 12 specialized micro-models that replace what would traditionally require a 0.6B parameter language model. Seven models face players (intent, sentiment, emotion, behavior, loot, dialogue ranking, embeddings) and five simulate the world.
facts: Uses sklearn LogisticRegression and TF-IDF pipelines
Zero GPU required for any model
All 12 models combined are smaller than a single web page image
Each model is trained on domain-specific data under 1 second

entries.description: A Character Genome is the complete definition of an NPC character in the Synthesus system, consisting of up to four JSON files: bio.json (identity), patterns.json (dialogue), knowledge.json (entity knowledge), and personality.json (creative responses).
facts: Every character is defined by a directory of JSON files
The bio.json file is the only required file
Patterns define trigger-response pairs for the Left Hemisphere
Knowledge graphs store what the NPC personally knows about the world

entries.description: Moonpetal flowers are delicate, silver-petaled blooms that only open under moonlight. They're the primary ingredient in healing potions and various medicinal tinctures. Found in shaded forest clearings and cultivated by herbalists.
facts: Petals dissolve instantly in sunlight
Must be harvested between midnight and dawn
A single flower yields enough extract for three basic healing potions
Wild moonpetals are more potent than cultivated ones

entries.description: Frostbloom is an extremely rare arctic flower that only blooms during the harshest winter storms. Its tincture was the cure for the devastating Winter Fever that swept the region five years ago. [entity] brought [emotion] hope during the [topic] crisis.
facts: Only grows above the snowline on the highest peaks
The herbalist Thessaly discovered its curative properties
A single bloom can cure a dozen fever victims
Attempts to cultivate it at lower altitudes have all failed

entries.description: Duke Aldric rules Ironhaven and the surrounding territory. A pragmatic but increasingly strained leader, he maintains order through alliances with the Merchant's Alliance and his personal guard. Rumors suggest his coffers are thinner than he lets on. [entity] maintains [emotion] control over [topic] matters.
facts: Has ruled for 22 years
His court robes are made from Silvermoor enchanted silk
Tariff agreements with the Alliance fund most city services
Recently took loans from foreign interests

emotion_variants.suspicious: *lowers voice* The duke has been acting strange lately. Quiet. His guards ask too many questions around the market.
